moduleDescription:
Statistical analysis framework for performing basic statistical analysis of data. The data is analysed in a single pass, when it is pushed to a RunningStat or RunningRegress object.

RunningStat calculates for a single data set

  • n (data count)
  • min (smallest value)
  • max (largest value)
  • sum
  • mean
  • variance
  • varianceS (sample variance)
  • standardDeviation
  • standardDeviationS (sample standard deviation)
  • skewness (the third statistical moment)
  • kurtosis (the fourth statistical moment)

RunningRegress calculates for two sets of data

  • n (data count)
  • slope
  • intercept
  • correlation

Procs are provided to calculate statistics on openArrays.

However, if more than a single statistical calculation is required, it is more efficient to push the data once to a RunningStat object and then call the numerous statistical procs for the RunningStat object:

entries.name: RunningStat
entries.code:
RunningStat = object
  n*: int                    ## amount of pushed data
  min*, max*, sum*: float    ## self-explaining
entries.description: An accumulator for statistical data.

entries.name: RunningRegress
entries.code:
RunningRegress = object
  n*: int                    ## amount of pushed data
  x_stats*: RunningStat      ## stats for the first set of data
  y_stats*: RunningStat      ## stats for the second set of data
entries.description: An accumulator for regression calculations.

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear(s: var RunningStat) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Resets s.

entries.name: push
entries.code: proc push(s: var RunningStat; x: float) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Pushes a value x for processing.

entries.name: push
entries.code: proc push(s: var RunningStat; x: int) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Pushes a value x for processing.

x is simply converted to float and the other push operation is called.

entries.name: push
entries.code: proc push(s: var RunningStat; x: openArray[float | int])
entries.description:
Pushes all values of x for processing.

Int values of x are simply converted to float and the other push operation is called.

entries.name: mean
entries.code: proc mean(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current mean of s.

entries.name: variance
entries.code: proc variance(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current population variance of s.

entries.name: varianceS
entries.code: proc varianceS(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current sample variance of s.

entries.name: standardDeviation
entries.code:
proc standardDeviation(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current population standard deviation of s.

entries.name: standardDeviationS
entries.code:
proc standardDeviationS(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current sample standard deviation of s.

entries.name: skewness
entries.code: proc skewness(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current population skewness of s.

entries.name: skewnessS
entries.code: proc skewnessS(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current sample skewness of s.

entries.name: kurtosis
entries.code: proc kurtosis(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current population kurtosis of s.

entries.name: kurtosisS
entries.code: proc kurtosisS(s: RunningStat): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current sample kurtosis of s.

entries.code: proc `+`(a, b: RunningStat): RunningStat {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Combines two RunningStats.

Useful when performing parallel analysis of data series and needing to re-combine parallel result sets.

entries.code:
proc `+=`(a: var RunningStat; b: RunningStat) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds the RunningStat b to a.

entries.code: proc `$`(a: RunningStat): string {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Produces a string representation of the RunningStat. The exact format is currently unspecified and subject to change. Currently it contains:
  • the number of probes
  • min, max values
  • sum, mean and standard deviation.

entries.code: proc mean[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the mean of x.

entries.name: variance
entries.code: proc variance[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the population variance of x.

entries.name: varianceS
entries.code: proc varianceS[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the sample variance of x.

entries.name: standardDeviation
entries.code: proc standardDeviation[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the population standard deviation of x.

entries.name: standardDeviationS
entries.code: proc standardDeviationS[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the sample standard deviation of x.

entries.name: skewness
entries.code: proc skewness[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the population skewness of x.

entries.name: skewnessS
entries.code: proc skewnessS[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the sample skewness of x.

entries.name: kurtosis
entries.code: proc kurtosis[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the population kurtosis of x.

entries.name: kurtosisS
entries.code: proc kurtosisS[T](x: openArray[T]): float
entries.description: Computes the sample kurtosis of x.

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear(r: var RunningRegress) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Resets r.

entries.name: push
entries.code:
proc push(r: var RunningRegress; x, y: float) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Pushes two values x and y for processing.

entries.name: push
entries.code:
proc push(r: var RunningRegress; x, y: int) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Pushes two values x and y for processing.

x and y are converted to float and the other push operation is called.

entries.name: push
entries.code: proc push(r: var RunningRegress; x, y: openArray[float | int])
entries.description: Pushes two sets of values x and y for processing.

entries.name: slope
entries.code: proc slope(r: RunningRegress): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current slope of r.

entries.name: intercept
entries.code: proc intercept(r: RunningRegress): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current intercept of r.

entries.name: correlation
entries.code: proc correlation(r: RunningRegress): float {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Computes the current correlation of the two data sets pushed into r.

entries.code:
proc `+`(a, b: RunningRegress): RunningRegress {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Combines two RunningRegress objects.

Useful when performing parallel analysis of data series and needing to re-combine parallel result sets

entries.code:
proc `+=`(a: var RunningRegress; b: RunningRegress) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds the RunningRegress b to a.
